You are working on the thunderbird/thunderbird-android repository.
The codebase is at version 11.0 (commit: 058c23f6).

## Issue to Resolve

Bump AndroidX Activity
Bump AndroidX Activity to 1.10.1 and replace custom `LocalActivity` by new official `LocalActivity`

See: https://developer.android.com/jetpack/androidx/releases/activity#1.10.1

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.

Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

content.thinking:
**Investigating LocalActivity**

I see the release notes talking about "New LocalActivity provides Activity." It probably refers to `ComponentActivity`, right? And I think `activity-compose` already uses `ComponentActivity`. So, maybe it's similar to our custom setup but with `Activity?` for previews? Iâ€™m considering adopting this with a safe-cast or `requireNotNull`. I should definitely check the files using `LocalActivity.current` to see what type they expect. I'll need to open them up for a closer look.

16â†’@Composable

19â†’    onBack: () -> Unit,
20â†’    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
21â†’    accountSetupFinishedLauncher: AccountSetupFinishedLauncher = koinInject(),

1â†’package app.k9mail.feature.funding.googleplay.ui.contribution
 2â†’
 3â†’import android.content.Intent
 4â†’import android.net.Uri
 5â†’import androidx.activity.compose.BackHandler
 6â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
 7â†’import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
 8â†’import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
 9â†’import androidx.compose.ui.res.stringResource
10â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.common.activity.LocalActivity
11â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.common.mvi.observe
12â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.designsystem.organism.TopAppBarWithBackButton
13â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.designsystem.template.Scaffold
14â†’import app.k9mail.feature.funding.googleplay.R
15â†’import app.k9mail.feature.funding.googleplay.ui.contribution.ContributionContract.ViewModel
16â†’import org.koin.androidx.compose.koinViewModel
17â†’
18â†’@Composable
19â†’internal fun ContributionScreen(
20â†’    onBack: () -> Unit,
21â†’    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
22â†’    viewModel: ViewModel = koinViewModel<ContributionViewModel>(),
23â†’) {
24â†’    val activity = LocalActivity.current
25â†’    val context = LocalContext.current
26â†’
27â†’    val (state, dispatch) = viewModel.observe { effect ->
28â†’        when (effect) {
29â†’            is ContributionContract.Effect.ManageSubscription -> {
30â†’                context.startActivity(
31â†’                    getManageSubscriptionIntent(
32â†’                        productId = effect.productId,
33â†’                        packageName = context.packageName,
34â†’                    ),
35â†’                )
36â†’            }
37â†’
38â†’            is ContributionContract.Effect.PurchaseContribution -> {
39â†’                effect.startPurchaseFlow(activity)
40â†’            }
41â†’        }
42â†’    }
43â†’
44â†’    BackHandler {
45â†’        onBack()
46â†’    }
47â†’
48â†’    Scaffold(
49â†’        modifier = modifier,
50â†’        topBar = {
51â†’            TopAppBarWithBackButton(
52â†’                title = stringResource(R.string.funding_googleplay_contribution_title),
53â†’                onBackClick = onBack,
54â†’            )
55â†’        },
56â†’    ) { innerPadding ->
57â†’        ContributionContent(
58â†’            state = state.value,
59â†’            onEvent = { dispatch(it) },
60â†’            contentPadding = innerPadding,
61â†’        )
62â†’    }
63â†’}
64â†’
65â†’private const val SUBSCRIPTION_URL = "https://play.google.com/store/account/subscriptions"
66â†’
67â†’private fun getManageSubscriptionIntent(
68â†’    productId: String,
69â†’    packageName: String,
70â†’): Intent {
71â†’    val uri = Uri.parse(SUBSCRIPTION_URL)
72â†’        .buildUpon()
73â†’        .appendQueryParameter("sku", productId)
74â†’        .appendQueryParameter("package", packageName)
75â†’        .build()
76â†’
77â†’    return Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri)
78â†’}
79â†’

1â†’package app.k9mail.feature.account.server.settings.ui.common
  2â†’
  3â†’import android.view.WindowManager
  4â†’import androidx.biometric.BiometricManager
  5â†’import androidx.biometric.BiometricPrompt
  6â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
  7â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.DisposableEffect
  8â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
  9â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.mutableStateOf
 10â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.saveable.rememberSaveable
 11â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.setValue
 12â†’import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
 13â†’import androidx.fragment.app.FragmentActivity
 14â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.common.activity.LocalActivity
 15â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.designsystem.atom.textfield.TextFieldOutlinedPassword
 16â†’
 17â†’/**
 18â†’ * Variant of [TextFieldOutlinedPassword] that only allows the password to be unmasked after the user has authenticated
 19â†’ * using [BiometricPrompt].
 20â†’ *
 21â†’ * Note: Due to limitations of [BiometricPrompt] this composable can only be used inside a [FragmentActivity].
 22â†’ */
 23â†’@Suppress("LongParameterList")
 24â†’@Composable
 25â†’fun TextFieldOutlinedPasswordBiometric(
 26â†’    value: String,
 27â†’    onValueChange: (String) -> Unit,
 28â†’    authenticationTitle: String,
 29â†’    authenticationSubtitle: String,
 30â†’    needScreenLockMessage: String,
 31â†’    onWarningChange: (CharSequence?) -> Unit,
 32â†’    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
 33â†’    label: String? = null,
 34â†’    isEnabled: Boolean = true,
 35â†’    isReadOnly: Boolean = false,
 36â†’    isRequired: Boolean = false,
 37â†’    hasError: Boolean = false,
 38â†’) {
 39â†’    var isPasswordVisible by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(false) }
 40â†’    var isAuthenticated by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(false) }
 41â†’    var isAuthenticationRequired by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(true) }
 42â†’
 43â†’    // If the entire password was removed, we allow the user to unmask the text field without requiring authentication.
 44â†’    if (value.isEmpty()) {
 45â†’        isAuthenticationRequired = false
 46â†’    }
 47â†’
 48â†’    val activity = LocalActivity.current as FragmentActivity
 49â†’
 50â†’    TextFieldOutlinedPassword(
 51â†’        value = value,
 52â†’        onValueChange = onValueChange,
 53â†’        modifier = modifier,
 54â†’        label = label,
 55â†’        isEnabled = isEnabled,
 56â†’        isReadOnly = isReadOnly,
 57â†’        isRequired = isRequired,
 58â†’        hasError = hasError,
 59â†’        isPasswordVisible = isPasswordVisible,
 60â†’        onPasswordVisibilityToggleClicked = {
 61â†’            if (!isAuthenticationRequired || isAuthenticated) {
 62â†’                isPasswordVisible = !isPasswordVisible
 63â†’                activity.setSecure(isPasswordVisible)
 64â†’            } else {
 65â†’                showBiometricPrompt(
 66â†’                    activity,
 67â†’                    authenticationTitle,
 68â†’                    authenticationSubtitle,
 69â†’                    needScreenLockMessage,
 70â†’                    onAuthSuccess = {
 71â†’                        isAuthenticated = true
 72â†’                        isPasswordVisible = true
 73â†’                        onWarningChange(null)
 74â†’                        activity.setSecure(true)
 75â†’                    },
 76â†’                    onAuthError = onWarningChange,
 77â†’                )
 78â†’            }
 79â†’        },
 80â†’    )
 81â†’
 82â†’    DisposableEffect(key1 = "secureWindow") {
 83â†’        activity.setSecure(isPasswordVisible)
 84â†’
 85â†’        onDispose {
 86â†’            activity.setSecure(false)
 87â†’        }
 88â†’    }
 89â†’}
 90â†’
 91â†’private fun showBiometricPrompt(
 92â†’    activity: FragmentActivity,
 93â†’    title: String,
 94â†’    subtitle: String,
 95â†’    needScreenLockMessage: String,
 96â†’    onAuthSuccess: () -> Unit,
 97â†’    onAuthError: (CharSequence) -> Unit,
 98â†’) {
 99â†’    val authenticationCallback = object : BiometricPrompt.AuthenticationCallback() {
100â†’        override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: BiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
101â†’            onAuthSuccess()
102â†’        }
103â†’
104â†’        override fun onAuthenticationError(errorCode: Int, errString: CharSequence) {
105â†’            if (errorCode == BiometricPrompt.ERROR_HW_NOT_PRESENT ||
106â†’                errorCode == BiometricPrompt.ERROR_NO_DEVICE_CREDENTIAL ||
107â†’                errorCode == BiometricPrompt.ERROR_NO_BIOMETRICS
108â†’            ) {
109â†’                onAuthError(needScreenLockMessage)
110â†’            } else if (errString.isNotEmpty()) {
111â†’                onAuthError(errString)
112â†’            }
113â†’        }

If `K9Activity` uses the `setContent` method from `androidx.activity.compose.setContent`, it may provide composition locals like `LocalOnBackPressedDispatcherOwner`. If `LocalActivity`'s type is `Activity?` or `ComponentActivity?`, I need to pass a mock of the correct type. For the official `LocalActivity`, I might need to mock `android.app.Activity` or `ComponentActivity`. Iâ€™ll specify the mock explicitly to prevent issues with Kotlin generics. However, in this test, it's unclear if the activity is used since just pressing back triggers the `onBack` event. 

The `ContributionScreen` retrieves the current activity for effect, but it seems like that effect isn't triggering. I should ensure there's a non-null activity available; otherwise, if `LocalActivity` defaults to null, it could crash. So, I might need to implement `val activity = LocalActivity.current` in app code to use properly in various branches.**Considering LocalActivity behavior**

If `LocalActivity` has a default that is null, I can technically assign null, but then the `startPurchaseFlow(activity)` effect might expect a non-null `Activity`. That could lead to compile errors if I leave it nullable. Itâ€™s best to assume itâ€™s non-null for simplicity. 

I can check if `LocalActivity` is defined as a non-null type, likely set up as a `staticCompositionLocalOf<Activity>()` method that throws an error if not provided. I'll proceed under the assumption that it is indeed a non-null `Activity`. In that case, I should consider using `android.app.Activity` or `ComponentActivity`. 

Additionally, I'll need to reference `LocalActivity` in the Detekt configuration as an allowed function for the `CompositionLocalProvider`. Iâ€™ll find the right spot to do that, around line 800.

content.thinking:
**Evaluating dependency updates**

I'm trying to figure out if the new activity version will change the transitive list. Just to be safe, I'll consider running `./gradlew dependencyGuard` to update files, even though that can be resource-intensive. The dependency files are likely needed by the Dropbox plugin, which expects an exact `runtimeClasspath`. Bumping the version might lead to more changes. We should ensure the txt files include the correct version, and maybe update the `app-thunderbird` and `app-k9mail` dependencies accordingly.**Finalizing code updates**

The new activity version 1.10.1 might change some transitive dependencies, especially with `activity-compose` likely depending on a new compose runtime version. I need to make sure that the `detekt.yml` allowlist includes the official LocalActivity. Some modules with composition locals may have issues, but it seems fine. I also noticed that `LocalActivity.kt` doesn't export LocalActivity, which could cause conflicts, but the package name is okay. Iâ€™ve ensured that the appropriate references to `staticCompositionLocal` are removed, and I didn't find any stray custom LocalActivity instances. I'm ready to summarize this!

content.text:
Implemented AndroidX Activity bump + migrated to the official `LocalActivity`.

Changes made:
- Updated `androidxActivity` to **1.10.1** in `gradle/libs.versions.toml`.
- Removed the custom `LocalActivity` from `core/ui/compose/common/.../activity/LocalActivity.kt`; `setActivityContent()` now just delegates to `setContent`.
- Replaced usages/imports of `app.k9mail...LocalActivity` with `androidx.activity.compose.LocalActivity` and handled the new **nullable** `LocalActivity.current` via `requireNotNull(...)`.
- Added `implementation(libs.androidx.activity.compose)` where needed (`feature/launcher`, `feature/funding/googleplay`, `feature/settings/import`, `feature/account/server/settings`).
- Updated dependency-guard runtime classpath snapshots to reflect `androidx.activity:*` **1.10.1**.

Verified by compiling the affected modules with Gradle.

1â†’package app.k9mail.feature.funding.googleplay.ui.contribution
 2â†’
 3â†’import android.content.Intent
 4â†’import android.net.Uri
 5â†’import androidx.activity.compose.BackHandler
 6â†’import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
 7â†’import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
 8â†’import androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext
 9â†’import androidx.compose.ui.res.stringResource
10â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.common.activity.LocalActivity
11â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.common.mvi.observe
12â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.designsystem.organism.TopAppBarWithBackButton
13â†’import app.k9mail.core.ui.compose.designsystem.template.Scaffold
14â†’import app.k9mail.feature.funding.googleplay.R
15â†’import app.k9mail.feature.funding.googleplay.ui.contribution.ContributionContract.ViewModel
16â†’import org.koin.androidx.compose.koinViewModel
17â†’
18â†’@Composable
19â†’internal fun ContributionScreen(
20â†’    onBack: () -> Unit,
21â†’    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
22â†’    viewModel: ViewModel = koinViewModel<ContributionViewModel>(),
23â†’) {
24â†’    val activity = LocalActivity.current
25â†’    val context = LocalContext.current
26â†’
27â†’    val (state, dispatch) = viewModel.observe { effect ->
28â†’        when (effect) {
29â†’            is ContributionContract.Effect.ManageSubscription -> {
30â†’                context.startActivity(
31â†’                    getManageSubscriptionIntent(
32â†’                        productId = effect.productId,
33â†’                        packageName = context.packageName,
34â†’                    ),
35â†’                )
36â†’            }
37â†’
38â†’            is ContributionContract.Effect.PurchaseContribution -> {
39â†’                effect.startPurchaseFlow(activity)
40â†’            }
41â†’        }
42â†’    }
43â†’
44â†’    BackHandler {
45â†’        onBack()
46â†’    }
47â†’
48â†’    Scaffold(
49â†’        modifier = modifier,
50â†’        topBar = {
51â†’            TopAppBarWithBackButton(
52â†’                title = stringResource(R.string.funding_googleplay_contribution_title),
53â†’                onBackClick = onBack,
54â†’            )
55â†’        },
56â†’    ) { innerPadding ->
57â†’        ContributionContent(
58â†’            state = state.value,
59â†’            onEvent = { dispatch(it) },
60â†’            contentPadding = innerPadding,
61â†’        )
62â†’    }
63â†’}
64â†’
65â†’private const val SUBSCRIPTION_URL = "https://play.google.com/store/account/subscriptions"
66â†’
67â†’private fun getManageSubscriptionIntent(
68â†’    productId: String,
69â†’    packageName: String,
70â†’): Intent {
71â†’    val uri = Uri.parse(SUBSCRIPTION_URL)
72â†’        .buildUpon()
73â†’        .appendQueryParameter("sku", productId)
74â†’        .appendQueryParameter("package", packageName)
75â†’        .build()
76â†’
77â†’    return Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW, uri)
78â†’}
79â†’

 34â†’    isEnabled: Boolean = true,
 35â†’    isReadOnly: Boolean = false,
 36â†’    isRequired: Boolean = false,
 37â†’    hasError: Boolean = false,
 38â†’) {
 39â†’    var isPasswordVisible by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(false) }
 40â†’    var isAuthenticated by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(false) }
 41â†’    var isAuthenticationRequired by rememberSaveable { mutableStateOf(true) }
 42â†’
 43â†’    // If the entire password was removed, we allow the user to unmask the text field without requiring authentication.
 44â†’    if (value.isEmpty()) {
 45â†’        isAuthenticationRequired = false
 46â†’    }
 47â†’
 48â†’    val activity = LocalActivity.current as FragmentActivity
 49â†’
 50â†’    TextFieldOutlinedPassword(
 51â†’        value = value,
 52â†’        onValueChange = onValueChange,
 53â†’        modifier = modifier,
 54â†’        label = label,
 55â†’        isEnabled = isEnabled,
 56â†’        isReadOnly = isReadOnly,
 57â†’        isRequired = isRequired,
 58â†’        hasError = hasError,
 59â†’        isPasswordVisible = isPasswordVisible,
 60â†’        onPasswordVisibilityToggleClicked = {
 61â†’            if (!isAuthenticationRequired || isAuthenticated) {
 62â†’                isPasswordVisible = !isPasswordVisible
 63â†’                activity.setSecure(isPasswordVisible)
 64â†’            } else {
 65â†’                showBiometricPrompt(
 66â†’                    activity,
 67â†’                    authenticationTitle,
 68â†’                    authenticationSubtitle,
 69â†’                    needScreenLockMessage,
 70â†’                    onAuthSuccess = {
 71â†’                        isAuthenticated = true
 72â†’                        isPasswordVisible = true
 73â†’                        onWarningChange(null)
 74â†’                        activity.setSecure(true)
 75â†’                    },
 76â†’                    onAuthError = onWarningChange,
 77â†’                )
 78â†’            }
 79â†’        },
 80â†’    )
 81â†’
 82â†’    DisposableEffect(key1 = "secureWindow") {
 83â†’        activity.setSecure(isPasswordVisible)
 84â†’
 85â†’        onDispose {
 86â†’            activity.setSecure(false)
 87â†’        }
 88â†’    }
 89â†’}
 90â†’
 91â†’private fun showBiometricPrompt(
 92â†’    activity: FragmentActivity,
 93â†’    title: String,
 94â†’    subtitle: String,
 95â†’    needScreenLockMessage: String,
 96â†’    onAuthSuccess: () -> Unit,
 97â†’    onAuthError: (CharSequence) -> Unit,
 98â†’) {
 99â†’    val authenticationCallback = object : BiometricPrompt.AuthenticationCallback() {
100â†’        override fun onAuthenticationSucceeded(result: BiometricPrompt.AuthenticationResult) {
101â†’            onAuthSuccess()
102â†’        }
103â†’
104â†’        override fun onAuthenticationError(errorCode: Int, errString: CharSequence) {
105â†’            if (errorCode == BiometricPrompt.ERROR_HW_NOT_PRESENT ||
106â†’                errorCode == BiometricPrompt.ERROR_NO_DEVICE_CREDENTIAL ||
107â†’                errorCode == BiometricPrompt.ERROR_NO_BIOMETRICS
108â†’            ) {
109â†’                onAuthError(needScreenLockMessage)
110â†’            } else if (errString.isNotEmpty()) {
111â†’                onAuthError(errString)
112â†’            }
113â†’        }
114â†’    }
115â†’
116â†’    val promptInfo = BiometricPrompt.PromptInfo.Builder()
117â†’        .setAllowedAuthenticators(
118â†’            BiometricManager.Authenticators.BIOMETRIC_STRONG or
119â†’                BiometricManager.Authenticators.BIOMETRIC_WEAK or
120â†’                BiometricManager.Authenticators.DEVICE_CREDENTIAL,
121â†’        )
122â†’        .setTitle(title)
123â†’        .setSubtitle(subtitle)
124â†’        .build()
125â†’
126â†’    BiometricPrompt(activity, authenticationCallback).authenticate(promptInfo)
127â†’}
128â†’
129â†’private fun FragmentActivity.setSecure(secure: Boolean) {
130â†’    window.setFlags(if (secure) WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_SECURE else 0, WindowManager.LayoutParams.FLAG_SECURE)
131â†’}
